sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient has been experiencing lower back pain for an extended period, with the pain radiating down his left leg.
He rates his pain as 6 out of 10, but it can reach up to 13 out of 10 when it gets worse.
The pain is exacerbated by standing, walking, coughing, and sneezing, but is relieved by laying down.
He has been unable to perform daily activities due to the pain, including tying his shoes.
The pain is described as an electrical toothache-like sensation that goes down the whole leg.
The patient has a history of a fall 30 years ago that may have resulted in a fracture or disk issue.
He has not been taking his prescribed medications.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of a fall 30 years ago that may have resulted in a fracture or disk issue.
He has been bedridden due to the pain.
Review of systems: The patient's lower back pain radiates down his left leg, causing weakness in both legs.
Current medications: Not specified.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not specified.
Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation at the L4 vertebrae.
He has pain with flexion and extension of his back.
He has 4 out of 5 strength in the left leg and 5 out of 5 strength in the right leg.
His reflexes are brisk and motor and sensory are intact in both lower extremities.
Test Results: The MRI shows a disk herniation at the level of L4-L5 vertebrae associated with nerve root impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement causing lower back pain and leg pain.
Problem list: Acute disk herniation at L4-L5 with nerve root impingement; lower back pain; leg pain.
Plan:
I will recommend an epidural steroid injection to place anti-inflammatory medication at the spot of inflammation.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the area.
The patient will see me again in about a month to follow up on the injection's effectiveness.
Follow-up: The patient will see me again in about a month to follow up on the injection's effectiveness.
